volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: Amphitryon (Section 1.1.344d - Section 1.1.390)

sections.label: Section 1.1.344d
sections.translation: Now you are telling a lie .

sections.label: Section 1.1.345
sections.original: At iam faciam ut verum dicas dicere .
sections.translation: But I ’ ll soon make you own that I ’ m telling the truth .

sections.label: Section 1.1.345b
sections.translation: What necessity is there for it ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.346
sections.original: Possum scire , quo profectus , cuius sis aut quid veneris ?
sections.translation: Can I know whence you have set out , whose you are , or why you are come ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.347
sections.original: Húc eo , eri iussu , eius sum servos . numquid nunc es certior ?
sections.translation: This way I ’ m going , and I ’ m the servant of my master . Are you any the wiser now ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.348
sections.original: Ego tibi istam hodie , sceleste , comprimam linguam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll this day make you be holding that foul tongue of yours .

sections.label: Section 1.1.348b
sections.translation: You can ’ t ; it is kept pure [*] and becomingly .

sections.label: Section 1.1.349b
sections.translation: Do you persist in chattering ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.350
sections.original: quid apud hasce aedis negoti ést tibi ?
sections.translation: What business now have you at this house ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.350b
sections.translation: Aye , and what business have you ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.351
sections.original: Rex Creo vigiles nocturnos singulos semper locat .
sections.translation: King Creon always sets a watch every night .

sections.label: Section 1.1.352
sections.original: Bene facit : quia nos eramus peregre , tutatust domi ;
sections.translation: He does right ; because we were abroad , he has been protecting our house . But however , do go in now , and say that some of the family servants have arrived .

sections.label: Section 1.1.354
sections.original: Nescio quam tu familiaris sis : nisi actutum hinc abis ,
sections.translation: How far you are one of the family servants I don ’ t know . But unless you are off from here this instant ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.355
sections.translation: family servant as you are , I ’ ll make you to be received in no familiar style .

sections.label: Section 1.1.356
sections.original: Hic inquam habito ego atque horunc servos sum .
sections.translation: Here , I say , I live , and of these people I am the servant .

sections.label: Section 1.1.356b
sections.translation: But do you understand how it is ? Unless you are off , I ’ ll make you to be exalted [*] this day .

sections.label: Section 1.1.357b
sections.translation: In what way , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.358
sections.original: Auferere , non abibis , sí ego fustem sumpsero .
sections.translation: You shall be carried off , you shan ’ t walk away , if I take up a stick .

sections.label: Section 1.1.359
sections.original: Quin me esse huius familiai familiarem praedico .
sections.translation: But I declare that I am one of the domestics of this family .

sections.label: Section 1.1.360
sections.original: Vide sis quam mox vapulare vis , nisi actutum hinc abis .
sections.translation: Consider , will you , how soon you want a drubbing , unless you are off from here this instant .

sections.label: Section 1.1.361
sections.original: Tun domo prohibere peregre me advenientem postulas ?
sections.translation: Do you want , as I arrive from foreign parts , to drive me from my home ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.362
sections.translation: Is this your home ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.362b
sections.translation: It is so , I say .

sections.label: Section 1.1.362c
sections.translation: Who is your master , then ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.363
sections.original: Amphitruo , qui nunc praefectust Thebanis legionibus ,
sections.translation: Amphitryon , who is now the general of the Theban forces , to whom Alcmena is married .

sections.label: Section 1.1.364b
sections.original: Quid ais ? quid nomen tibi est ?
sections.translation: How say you ? What ’ s your name ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.365
sections.original: Sosiam vocant Thebani , Davo prognatum patre .
sections.translation: The Thebans call me Sosia , the son of my father Davus .

sections.label: Section 1.1.366
sections.original: Ne tu istic hodie malo tuo compositis mendaciis
sections.translation: Assuredly , at your peril have you come here this day , with your trumped-up lies , your patched-up knaveries , you essence of effrontery .

sections.label: Section 1.1.368
sections.original: Immo equidem tunicis consutis huc advenio , non dolis .
sections.translation: Why no , it ’ s rather with garments patched-up that I ’ m arrived here , not with knaveries .

sections.label: Section 1.1.369
sections.original: At mentiris etiam : certo pedibus , non tunicis venis .
sections.translation: Why , you are lying again ; you come with your feet , surely , and not with your garments .

sections.label: Section 1.1.370
sections.translation: Yes , certainly .

sections.label: Section 1.1.370b
sections.translation: Then certainly take that for your lie .

sections.label: Section 1.1.371
sections.translation: By my troth , I certainly don ’ t wish for it of course .

sections.label: Section 1.1.371b
sections.translation: But by my faith , you certainly shall have it of course , whether you wish or not : for , in fact , this is certainly my determination , and it is not at your own option .

sections.label: Section 1.1.373
sections.translation: Mercy , I entreat of you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.373b
sections.original: Tun te audes Sosiam esse dicere ,
sections.translation: Do you dare to say that you are Sosia , when I myself am he ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.374b
sections.translation: I ’ m being murdered .

sections.label: Section 1.1.374c
sections.original: Parum etiam , praeut futurum est , praedicas .
sections.translation: Why , you are crying out for a trifle as yet , compared with what it will be .

sections.label: Section 1.1.375
sections.translation: Whose are you now ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.375b
sections.original: Tuos , nam pugnis usu fecisti tuom .
sections.translation: Your own ; for with your fists you have laid hands on me [*] . Help , help , citizens of Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1.1.376b
sections.translation: What , still bawling , you scoundrel ? Speak — what have you come for ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.377b
sections.original: Vt esset quem tu pugnis caederes .
sections.translation: For there to be somebody for you to belabour with your fists .

sections.label: Section 1.1.378
sections.translation: Whose are you ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.378b
sections.translation: Amphitryon ’ s Sosia , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.378c
sections.translation: For this reason then you shall be beaten the more , because you prate thus idly ; I am Sosia , not you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.380
sections.original: Ita di faciant , ut tu potius sis atque ego te ut verberem .
sections.translation: I wish the Gods would have it so , that you were he in preference , and that I were thrashing you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.381
sections.translation: What , muttering still ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.381b
sections.translation: I ’ ll hold my tongue then .

sections.label: Section 1.1.381c
sections.translation: Who is your master ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.381d
sections.translation: Whoever you like .

sections.label: Section 1.1.382
sections.translation: How then ? What ’ s your name now ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.382b
sections.translation: Nothing but what you shall command .

sections.label: Section 1.1.383
sections.translation: You said that you were Amphitryon ’ s Sosia .

sections.label: Section 1.1.383b
sections.translation: I made a mistake ; but this I meant to say , that I was Amphitryon ’ s associate [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.385
sections.original: Scibam equidem nullum esse nobis nisi me servom Sosiam .
sections.translation: Why , I was sure that we had no servant called Sosia except myself . Your senses are forsaking you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.386b
sections.translation: I wish that those fists of yours had done so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.387
sections.original: Ego sum Sosia ille quem tu dudum esse aiebas mihi .
sections.translation: I am that Sosia , whom you were just now telling me that you are .

sections.label: Section 1.1.388
sections.original: Obsecro ut per pacem liceat te alloqui , ut ne vapulem .
sections.translation: I pray that I may be allowed to discourse with you in quietness , so as not to be beaten .

sections.label: Section 1.1.389
sections.original: Immo indutiae parumper fiant , si quid vis loqui .
sections.translation: Well then , let there be a truce for a short time , if you want to say anything .

sections.label: Section 1.1.390
sections.original: Non loquar nisi pace facta , quando pugnis plus vales .
sections.translation: I ’ ll not speak unless peace is concluded , since you are the stronger with your fists .
